name: Minimum Acceptance Gate
purpose: Run only the durable verification command declared for the Change's primary Module.

oracle.description: The exact Git binding Interface tests must exit zero.

rejects: failed or truncated Git observations treated as exact

residualUncertainty: Non-current-host Git behavior is not exercised.

command: const observer = await import('./src/core/command-runner.mjs'); if (observer.COMMAND_OBSERVATION_PROOF_VERSION !== 1) throw new Error('Command Observation proof version 1 is not implemented.'); await import('./test/assurance-runtime/command-runner.test.mjs');

oracle.description: The fixed Command Observation proof version and its terminal-state conformance scenario must exit zero.

rejects: timeout or cancellation returned before close while late process effects remain possible
signal, start failure, unsupported support profile, malformed adapter output, or unconfirmed close synthesized as natural exit zero
stdout and stderr truncation or invalid UTF-8 losing their original byte counts and digests
captured text treated as the authoritative stream instead of bounded raw bytes

residualUncertainty: The declared profile is limited to direct argv on Linux or macOS under Node major 22, 24, or 25.
Descendants that deliberately escape the observed POSIX process group remain outside this proof.

oracle.description: Project Model validation plus compiled-scope and Outcome-alignment adversarial tests must exit zero.

rejects: dangling governance references
read or write scope broadening
implicit cross-Claim Evidence mapping
missing, unknown, or non-active Development Outcome references
unresolved or ambiguous exact Contract-visible Claim-to-Criterion Outcome Contributions
Outcome exception acceptance without frozen Development Plan authority

residualUncertainty: The current suite groups several tightly coupled compiler invariants in two Change Kernel scenarios.
A bound Outcome Contribution proves relevance only; Criterion satisfaction requires the separately compiled Outcome Transition proof and Plan-authority assessment.
Outcome Transition proof trusts the local single-writer Change Store; authenticated multi-writer history remains outside the current assurance boundary.

oracle.description: Acceptance lifecycle and adversarial integrity tests must exit zero.

rejects: self-ratifying or forged Evidence
stale or content-altered acceptance
out-of-scope and unclosed knowledge changes

residualUncertainty: Integration assurance and cross-Module HTTP behavior remain full-Gate concerns.

oracle.description: The CLI and loopback transport Interface tests must exit zero.

rejects: non-loopback or unbounded HTTP behavior
route-to-kernel mapping regressions
package-manager symlink entrypoint regressions

residualUncertainty: Browser DOM behavior and the real HTTP-to-Kernel lifecycle remain full-Gate concerns.

command: const protocol = await import('./src/worker-execution/protocol.mjs'); if (protocol.WORKER_EXECUTION_INTERFACE_PROOF_VERSION !== 1) throw new Error('Worker Execution Interface proof version 1 is not implemented.'); await import('./test/worker-execution/interface.test.mjs');

oracle.description: The fixed Worker Execution protocol version and its canonical lifecycle plus semantic attack table must exit zero.

rejects: malformed, over-limit, contradictory, reordered, stale, cross-execution, or post-terminal values
capability escalation or a Context Expansion Request granting its own scope
provider, model, session, transcript, source-body, or open extension payload leakage
Worker Report or Execution Record fields that claim Evidence, Knowledge Closure, Authority, Claim satisfaction, or acceptance

residualUncertainty: The proof is pure and spawns no real worker or provider Adapter.
Filesystem, process, network, and secret capabilities are not independently enforced until LGT-022.
Local Authority identities remain unauthenticated and Worker observations do not prove work correctness.

oracle.description: The fixed Context Kernel cross-Interface proof must accept exact initial materialization and recorded-before-disclosure expansion while every declared source, scope, settlement, lineage, authority, and resource attack exits nonzero inside the scenario.

rejects: initial materialization that includes bytes outside the logical Context Capsule read scope
missing, extra, drifted, ambiguous, escaping, symlink, unreadable, duplicate, or over-limit source entries
a Worker request that grants its own scope, successor Context, or Context Impact
stale, cross-execution, cross-source, replayed, broadened, partial, or impact-substituted settlement
new source-byte disclosure before the exact expansion ledger and blob state persist
Context lineage that changes write scope or creates capability, semantic-impact, Evidence, Knowledge Closure, Authority, or acceptance conclusions

residualUncertainty: The proof establishes the local Context delivery seam, not that a same-user Worker process cannot read the repository or acquire knowledge through another channel.
Raw filesystem read and write enforcement remains the worker-capability-enforcement-not-proven Gap and LGT-022 responsibility.
Real provider delivery, use of disclosed content, adapter parity, and intent-to-acceptance remain LGT-023 and LGT-024 responsibilities.
